verdict: Chain incomplete: WMI-1/2/3 cluster consistently on dmamove_job, postproc_job, and vgem_buf_state, but zero candidates confirm WMI-4 (control-flow hijack) anywhere in the evidence, so the chain cannot close.

kept.reason: Live-resource-persist evidence directly in postproc_job_submit, which operates on authoritative object postproc_job (WMI-1+3 co-located per object_roles) — same-function nucleus feed.
Second WMI-2 site in the same nucleus-feeding function postproc_job_submit; corroborates persistence of postproc_job across the call.
WMI-2 persistence site in dmamove_fetch_ring, an immediate dataflow step into authoritative object dmamove_job.
WMI-2 persistence directly in dmamove_job_execute, which operates on the dmamove_job nucleus (WMI-1+3+4 co-located structurally).

Second WMI-2 site in dmamove_job_exec corroborating job persistence on dmamove_job.
Signal divergence confirmed in vgem_dmamove_buf_state_get, which directly reads/writes authoritative object vgem_buf_state (WMI-1+3 co-located).
Additional WMI-1 site in the same nucleus-touching function; only-site confirmation for the signal-divergence role on vgem_buf_state.

Second WMI-1 site in the same nucleus-feeding function postproc_update_buf_state.

Corroborating WMI-1 site in postproc_job_submit.
WMI-1 divergence on nucleus function vgem_dmamove_buf_state_get touching vgem_buf_state.
Corroborating WMI-1 site in the same nucleus function.
Corroborating WMI-1 site in the same nucleus function.
Corroborating WMI-1 site in the same nucleus function, rounding out the vgem_buf_state divergence evidence.
Only confirmed WMI-3 (attacker content) site landing directly in the vgem_buf_state nucleus function — required to complete the co-located WMI-3 role on this authoritative object.
Second WMI-3 site in the same nucleus function, corroborating attacker-controlled content reaching vgem_buf_state.
Only confirmed WMI-3 site in postproc_job_submit — required to satisfy the attacker-content role on the postproc_job authoritative object.

chain_summary: Structural (static) evidence flags dmamove_job, postproc_job and vgem_buf_state as candidate nuclei since only these co-locate WMI-3 (and dmamove_job also WMI-4) roles. Symbolic execution concretely confirms WMI-1 (signal divergence) and WMI-2 (live persist) heavily in the feeder functions vgem_dmamove_buf_state_get, dmamove_fetch_ring, dmamove_job_execute, dmamove_job_exec, postproc_update_buf_state and postproc_job_submit, and confirms WMI-3 (attacker content reaching the resource) only twice, both landing directly on the nucleus (vgem_dmamove_buf_state_get, postproc_job_submit). However, the SE sweep shows zero confirmed WMI-4 (control-flow hijack) candidates anywhere, and the co-occurrence anchor reports chain_complete=False, wma_host=False. Per rule, an incomplete role set forces wma_confirmed=false regardless of the strength of roles 1-3. Of the 78 concrete-replay-kept candidates, 25 sit on or immediately feed the candidate nucleus and are kept; the remaining 53 belong to peripheral structs/functions (dmamove_ctx, dmamove_token_slot, move_desc, pipeline_ctx, pipeline_job, postproc_comp_entry, postproc_ctx, and unattributed WMI-2 sites) with no shown dataflow into the nucleus and are dropped as redundant with roles already confirmed there.
